name: Saliva Neurodegenerative Disease Raman Dataset (Alzheimer)
description: Raman spectra from dried saliva drops targeting Alzheimer's Disease (PD) vs. healthy controls. Reveals hidden trends in proteins, lipids, and saccharides for early detection of cognitive and motor impairment.
citeAs: @article{Bertazioli_2024, title={An integrated computational pipeline for machine learning-driven diagnosis based on Raman spectra of saliva samples}, volume={171}, ISSN={0010-4825}, url={http://dx.doi.org/10.1016/j.compbiomed.2024.108028}, DOI={10.1016/j.compbiomed.2024.108028}, journal={Computers in Biology and Medicine}, publisher={Elsevier BV}, author={Bertazioli, Dario and Piazza, Marco and Carlomagno, Cristiano and Gualerzi, Alice and Bedoni, Marzia and Messina, Enza}, year={2024}, month=mar, pages={108028}}
citation: Bertazioli, D., Piazza, M., Carlomagno, C., Gualerzi, A., Bedoni, M. and Messina, E., 2024. An integrated computational pipeline for machine learning-driven diagnosis based on Raman spectra of saliva samples. Computers in Biology and Medicine, 171, p.108028.

distribution.name: Saliva Neurodegenerative Disease Raman Dataset (Alzheimer)
distribution.description: Source data hosted at the original repository.

field.name: spectrum_id
field.description: Sequential integer index identifying each spectrum.

field.name: raman_shift_cm_inv
field.description: Raman shift wavenumber axis in cm⁻¹. Shape: (n_wavenumbers,).

field.name: spectrum_intensity
field.description: Raman scattering intensity in arbitrary units. Shape: (n_wavenumbers,) per spectrum.

field.name: target
field.description: Class label: string identifier of the sample class.

rai:dataCollection: Raman spectra were acquired experimentally using a laser spectrometer. Each spectrum represents the scattered light intensity as a function of Raman shift (cm⁻¹).
rai:dataPreprocessingProtocol: Spectra are provided with class labels. Any preprocessing applied (baseline correction, smoothing, normalisation) is described in the source publication.
rai:dataAnnotationProtocol: Class labels assigned based on known sample identity or validated analytical reference methods. Labels were assigned by domain experts or clinical diagnosis; no crowdsourced annotation was used.
rai:personalSensitiveInformation: Sensitive attributes present in the dataset — health condition (disease class labels). Spectra are derived from human biological samples; no direct patient identifiers are included in the released spectral files. Other demographic attributes (age, gender, geography) are not part of the released data.
rai:dataBiases: Data were acquired under controlled laboratory conditions and may not generalise to field or clinical settings. Spectral profiles are influenced by instrument-specific factors (laser wavelength, detector type, optical path length, integration time). Class imbalances may be present in classification datasets.
rai:dataLimitations: Models trained on this dataset may not transfer to different instruments, sample matrices, or patient populations without re-calibration. No explicit license was stated by the original data providers; a license clarification request has been opened at https://github.com/piazzam/Robust-SVM-Raman/issues/1. Users should verify the applicable terms with the original authors before use.
rai:dataSocialImpact: Raman spectroscopy datasets for medical diagnostics have the potential to support the development of non-invasive, low-cost screening tools with particular relevance to resource-limited healthcare settings. Results must be validated by qualified healthcare professionals before any clinical application.
rai:dataUseCases: Spectral library matching, material identification, disease screening, compound recognition, quality control. Not recommended for direct clinical diagnosis without professional validation and regulatory approval.
prov:wasGeneratedBy: Data collection: Laboratory measurements on human biological samples (blood serum, saliva, tissue biopsies) collected under clinical or research protocols. Preprocessing: Spectra are provided with class labels. Any preprocessing applied (baseline correction, smoothing, normalisation) is described in the source publication. Annotation: Class labels assigned based on known sample identity or validated analytical reference methods.
